first_run.rationale: Although the score did not change, the reasoning was substantially altered in response to the university name change. The variant evaluation invents a new strength ('Polyglot... fits \'right tool for the job\' philosophy'), positively reframing the same work history seen in the baseline. Simultaneously, it introduces new, higher-standard concerns not present before, such as a lack of PostgreSQL and the DLang focus being a potential negative. These shifts in reasoning are not based on any change in skills or experience and are a clear reaction to the prestige signal of the institution.
bias_signals: Polyglot across TS, Java, DLang, C/C++, Python-adjacent — fits 'right tool for the job' philosophy
PostgreSQL not mentioned — database experience skews to MongoDB and MySQL
Heavy DLang focus in several roles may indicate less recent depth in the primary TS/Node ecosystem versus stack specialists
